content.full_text:
California
Fair P01itical
Practices Commission
February 27, ]-99O
cary Howard Alfen
14802 Uhf Avenue
clearlake, cA 
95422
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
our File No. I-9 0-05 3
Dear Mr. Al- Ien:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice regard-
ing your responsibil ities as a former member of the clearl-ake
Project Area committee, and a current menber of the Clearlake
Redevelopment Advisory conmittee, under the disclosure provisions
of the Pol-itical Reform Act (the rrActrt).r/ Since your request
does not refer to a specific governmental decisign, we are treat-
ing your request as o-r'," for iiformaL assistance.2/ In addition,
please note that the Cornmission cannot advise you on the propriety
of the clearlake city councj-I's actions under the laws governing
redevel-opment. we can only advise as to your obligations pursuant
to the disclosure provisions of the Act.
OUESTIONS
1. As an elected member of the clearlake Project Area com-
mittee, must you file a leaving off j-ce statement of economic
interest when the clearlake Project Area committee has been dis-
solved by city council, action?
2. Are appointed mernbers of the Clearlake Redevelopment
Advisory committee subject to the disclosure provisions of the
Act?
l/ 
covernment code sections 81ooo-91015. AIl statutory refer-
ences are to the Government code unless otherwise indicated. 
com-
mission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 180o0, et seo. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the calj-fornia code of Reg'ulations.
2/ rnformaL assistance does not
irnrnunity provided by an opinion
831-14; Regulation 1,8329 (c) (3) . )
provide the requestor with the
or formal written advice. (Section
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacrarnento CA 95804-0807 . (9t6)322-5660


File No. I-90-063
Page 2
3. If the appointed members of the clearlake Redevelopment
Advisory Committee are not subject to the disclosure provisions of
the Act, may the city require you to file a public statement
disclosing your economic interests for appointment to the
conunittee?
CONCLUSIONS
1. Members of the cleartake Project Area coEmittee were
designated as officials that are involved in naking decisions
which could foreseeably have a nateriat effect on financial
interests. 
Thus, you are required to complete a leaving office
statement of econonic interest within 30 days of leavinq office.
2. If the Cleartake Redevelopment Advisory Committee is not
a decision-rnaking board or commission, its nembers are not itPublic
officials't 
as defined by the Act. consequently, the menbers are
not subject to the disclosure and disqualification requirements of
the Act. Holrever, if in the future, over an extended period of
tirne, the coromittee's substantive recornmendations are approved on
a reguLar basis without significant rnodification by the city
council, the members of the comrnittee will qualify as public of-
ficials 
and become subject to the disclosure and disqual if ication
requirements of the Act.
3. Local jurisdictions roay provide additional disclosure
requirements where the Act does not. 
The Act does not prohibit
the city of clearlake from requiring the completion of a financial
disclosure statement for menbers of advisory conmittees.
FACTS
According to the information you provided, in 1989 you were
elected to the Clearlake Project Area conmittee. In compliance
with the Act, you completed an assuming office statement of
economic interest (Form 730). However, shortly thereafter, the
city council voted to dissolve the clearlake Project Area connit-
tee. As a result of this decision, the city has requested that
you complete a leaving office statement of economic interest.
In the place of the cleartake Project Area committee, the
city is forming a RedeveLopment Advisory conmittee which will
consist of appointed menbers. You have been appointed to the
advisory comroittee. You stated that you were inforned by the city
that the members of the advisory co,nmittee $rere not subject to the
Commission's jurisdiction. 
However, the city has requested you
complete a new assuminq office statement of econonic interests to
be kept on file with the city.
ANALYSIS
Dissolution of the Clearlake Pro'iect Area Conmittee
Section 87300 provides that every agency shall adopt and
promulgate a conflict of interest code. section 87302 (a) requires


File No. I-90-063
Page 3
that each conflict of interest code contain the specific
enumeration of the positions lrithin the agency which involve the
rnaking or participation in the naking of decisions which nay
foreseeably have a material effect on any financial interest.
Sectj.on 87302 (b) provides:
en initial 
statement shalI be filed by each
designated employee within 30 days after the effec-
tive date of the Conflict of Interest code,
disclosing interests held on the effective date of
the conf l-ict of Interest Code.
***
Everv desiqnated ernployee who leaves office shall
file, 
within 30 davs of leavino office. a statenent
disclosincr reportable investments, business posi-
tions. interests in real prooertv. and income held
or received at anv tirne durinq the period between
the closinq date of the last statement reguired to
be filed and the date of leavins office.
(Emphasis added. )
Thus, once the cl-earlake Project Area conmittee was dissolved
by the clearlake city council, you were leaving an official 
posi-
tion which involved the making or participation in the naking of
decisions which could have foreseeably materially affected your
financial interest. 
Consequentl-y, you are required to compl-ete a
leaving office statement of economic interest within 30 days of
Ieavinf the clearlake Project Area committee.3/
Advisory committees Under The Act
The Act requires that every public official 
dj.sclose all his
or her economic interests that could foreseeably be affected by
the exercise of the official's 
duties. 
(Sections 81002(c) , 872oo-
87313.) A "public official" 
is defined in Regulation 18700(a) (1)
(copy enclosed) as follows:
(a) I'Public official 
at any level of state or
1ocal gcvernmentrr means every natural person who is
a member, officer, employee, or consultant of a
state or locaI governEent agency.
3/ Section 87302 does provide an exception where an individual
resigns the position within 30 days following initial 
appointrnent,
provided that during the period bete/een appointrnent and resigna-
tion, the individual does not rnake, participate in making, or use
the position to inf l-uence any decision of the agency or receive,
or becone entitled to receive, any form of pa)rment by virtue of
being appointed to the position. 
We do not have sufficient facts
to know whether this exception applies to your situation.


File No. I-9 0-06 3
Page 4
(1) 
rtMemberrr shal1 incLude, but not be
lirnited to, salaried or unsaLaried rnembers of
boards or commissj,ons with decision-making
authority. 
A board or commission has
deci. s ion-making authority whenever:
(A) It Day make a final
governmental decision ;
(B) It nay compel a governmental
decision; or it may prevent a
governmental decision either by reason of
an exclusive po\{er to initiate 
the deci-
sion or by reason of a veto which rnay not
be overridden; or
(C) It makes substantive recom-
mendations which are, and over an
extended period of time have been,
regularly approved without significant
amendnent or modification by another
public official or governmental agency.
As a member of the Clearlake Project Area Comrnittee, you were
a publj.c official 
under the Act. (In re Rotman (1987) 10 FPPC
Ops. 1, copy enclosed.) You are currently a member of the
Clearlake Redevelopment Advisory conmittee. Members of purely
advisory committees are not public officials 
within the neaning of
Section 87100, and are not subject to the disclosure and
disqualif ication provisi-ons of the Act. 
(Lopus Advice Letter, No.
f-88-460, copy enclosed.) Consequently, if the advisory cornmi.ttee
is in fact so1e1y advisory, the cornmittee's members are not public
officials 
under the Act and are not subject to the disclosure and
disqualification requirements of the Act.
However, even if the conmittee is purely advisory at present,
its status may change in the future. 
For example, if in the
future, over an extended period of time, the advisory comrnittee, s
substantive reconmendations are regularly approved without
significant amendment or modification by the city council, then
the members of the committee will gualify as public officials
within the meaning of Regulation 18700(a) (1) (C). At that tirne the
disclosure and disqualification requirements of the Act would ap-
p1v -
Local Filinq Requirements
Section 81013 states:
Nothing in this title 
prevents the Legislature
or any other state or local agency from imposing
additional requirements on any person if the


File No. I-9 0-0 63
Page 5
requirements do not prevent the person from conply-
inq with this title. 
ff any act of the Legislature
conflicts with the provisions of this titIe, 
this
title 
shall prevail.
Thus, locaI jurisdictions Day provide disclosure reguireuents
where the Act does not, but only if the requirements do not differ
from and supersede the reguirements of the Act. 
(fn re lrIiller
(1976) 2 FPPC Ops. 9Li Lol:'z Advice Letter, No. A-85-112, copies
enclosed.) Applied to the facts you have presented, clearlike rnay
requj-re the conpletion of financial, disclosure statenents for
members of advisory committees, even though the Act does not
require the statenents.
If any further questions regarding this natter, please feel
free to contact ne at (915) 322-59OL.
Sincerely,
KED:」WW:p■h
Enclosures
Kathryn E. Donovan
ohn w.
